info.name: Travel-Tips-And-Guideline
info.description:
# Travel Share Guideline API

Backend API for a social travel platform where users can share travel experiences, create travel plans, interact with posts, and collaborate with other travelers.

## Base URL

`http://localhost:5000/api/v1`

## Authentication

Most endpoints require a JWT access token.

Header format:

`Authorization: Bearer accessToken`

body.raw:
{ "title": "Exploring the Mountains of Nepal", "content": "

There’s something magical about leaving behind the endless horns and crowded streets of Dhaka and heading toward the calm, green landscapes of Sylhet. The journey itself feels like a transition—from concrete and chaos to misty hills, tea gardens, and fresh air.


The Journey Begins

Our trip started before sunrise. The city was just waking up, with tea stalls opening and buses filling up with sleepy travelers. Whether you choose the train or a long-distance bus, the road out of Dhaka slowly replaces skyscrapers with open fields, rivers, and small village markets.

The further we traveled, the greener everything became. It felt like the air itself changed—cooler, calmer, and filled with the scent of rain and earth.


", "category": "Adventure", "locationName": "Bogura", "country": "Bangladesh", "travelDays": 3, "estimatedCost": 3000, "travelType": "budget", "image": "https://example.com/images/nepal-mountains.jpg", "premium": true, "bannerId": "698c128f8dece2b2e4bd0191", "contentIds": ["698c12a88dece2b2e4bd0194"] }

response.name: Get single post
response.body:
{ "success": true, "message": "Post retrieved successfully", "data": { "_id": "698c136e1495bf2659708a9e", "author": { "_id": "698a0e679025ee8a719505e0", "name": "Masud", "password": "", "profilePicture": "", "isVerified": false }, "title": "Exploring the Mountains of Nepal with Masud Rana", "content": "

There’s something magical about leaving behind the endless horns and crowded streets of Dhaka and heading toward the calm, green landscapes of Sylhet. The journey itself feels like a transition—from concrete and chaos to misty hills, tea gardens, and fresh air.


The Journey Begins

Our trip started before sunrise. The city was just waking up, with tea stalls opening and buses filling up with sleepy travelers. Whether you choose the train or a long-distance bus, the road out of Dhaka slowly replaces skyscrapers with open fields, rivers, and small village markets.

The further we traveled, the greener everything became. It felt like the air itself changed—cooler, calmer, and filled with the scent of rain and earth.



After several hours, we finally reached Sylhet. The first thing you notice is the slower pace of life. The streets are quieter, the sky seems wider, and the greenery surrounds you everywhere. Tea gardens stretch across the hills like soft green carpets, and the cool breeze feels like a reward after the long journey.

", "category": "Exploration", "image": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dnmvttdo4/image/upload/v1770787471/p8dygbqibbfyuoj6dr6l.jpg", "premium": true, "isDeleted": false, "createdAt": "2026-02-11T05:28:14.936Z", "updatedAt": "2026-03-01T10:17:25.959Z", "__v": 0, "upvoteCount": 0, "downvoteCount": 1, "travelType": "budget" }, "viewerContext": { "voteType": "none", "isOwner": true, "isSaved": true, "isFollowingAuthor": false } }
